name: Hobbies
description: Hobbies, pastimes, and the jargon, gear, and history behind them

questions.q: How many keys are on a standard modern piano?

source.notes: 52 white, 36 black; has been the standard since Steinway's 1880 model.

questions.q: In which sport do players hit a feathered projectile called a shuttlecock over a net?

source.notes: Modern rules codified in England in 1893.

questions.q: How many numbered sections are on a standard dartboard?

source.notes: Numbered 1-20 with a bullseye in the center.

questions.q: Which ancient civilization is credited with inventing the kite?

source.notes: Kites date back at least to the 5th century BC in Chinese records.

questions.q: Opposite sides on a standard six-sided die always add up to what number?

source.notes: 1 opposite 6, 2 opposite 5, 3 opposite 4.

questions.q: A 'toxophilite' is an enthusiast of which hobby?

source.notes: From Greek 'toxon' (bow) + 'philos' (loving).

questions.q: The hobby of spelunking involves exploring which natural feature?

source.notes: Known as 'caving' or 'potholing' in the UK.

questions.q: What was the original name Joseph Pilates gave the fitness discipline now known as Pilates?

source.notes: Pilates developed the system in internment during WWI.

questions.q: In what year was the Atari arcade classic Pong first released?

source.notes: Designed by Allan Alcorn; one of the first successful commercial video games.

questions.q: How many numbered slots (including 0 and 00) are on a standard American roulette wheel?

source.notes: European roulette has 37 slots (single 0 only).

questions.q: A person who studies or observes birds is called a(n) __?

source.notes: From Greek 'ornis' (bird) + 'logos' (study).

questions.q: The hobby of conchology involves collecting what?

source.notes: The scientific study of mollusks and their shells.

questions.q: Philately is the study and collection of what?

source.notes: The American Philatelic Society is the national organization.

questions.q: What is the total length of an American football field from end line to end line, including both end zones?

source.notes: 100-yard field of play plus two 10-yard end zones.

questions.q: The martial art of Taekwondo originated in which country?

source.notes: Recognized as an Olympic sport at Sydney 2000.

questions.q: What tool does one use to work yarn in the hobby of crochet?

source.notes: Distinct from knitting, which uses two needles.

questions.q: In what year did Apple release the first iPad?

source.notes: Launched April 3, 2010; iPhone launched in 2007.

questions.q: In photography, what does the abbreviation 'DOF' stand for?

source.notes: The range of distances from the camera that appear acceptably sharp.

questions.q: The scientific study and collection of insects is known as what?

source.notes: Herpetology = reptiles/amphibians; ichthyology = fish.

questions.q: The Japanese word 'karaoke' combines 'kara' (empty) with a shortened form of what English loanword?

source.notes: From 'ōkesutora' (orchestra); invented in Japan in the 1970s.

questions.q: Sherman Poppen's 1965 prototype of the snowboard was commercially sold under what name?

source.notes: Portmanteau of 'snow' and 'surfer'; coined by Poppen's wife.

questions.q: A 'numismatist' is someone who collects what?

source.notes: Also covers tokens, paper money, and medals.
